message:
fix: 20+ signature/behaviour mismatches caught by round 39 sweep

Three cycles of signature sweeps against inspect.signature(nx.*) turned up
a further wave of drop-in breakages. Grouped into three beads:

1. Round 39 cycle 1 (r39c1-01, HIGH) — naming mismatches exposed by kwarg
   calls (all raised TypeError before):
   - is_simple_path: second positional 'path' -> 'nodes' (nx)
   - is_isolate: 'node' -> 'n'
   - is_edge_cover: 'edges' -> 'cover'
   - all_neighbors: 'G' -> 'graph'
   - to_directed / to_undirected: 'G' -> 'graph'
   - restricted_view: 'nodes_to_remove'/'edges_to_remove' -> 'nodes'/'edges'
   - visibility_graph: 'sequence' -> 'series'
   - mycielskian: missing 'iterations' kwarg (iterated Mycielskians)
   - kosaraju_strongly_connected_components: missing 'source' kwarg AND
     yielded lists instead of sets (nx returns sets; isinstance check
     failed on fnx). Now yields sets via a generator wrapper.

2. Round 39 cycle 2 (r39c2-01, HIGH) — signatures + behaviour:
   - k_factor: missing matching_weight
   - node_attribute_xy: missing nodes
   - remove_edge_attributes / remove_node_attributes: signature was
     (G, name); nx takes (G, *attr_names, ebunch/nbunch=None). Multi-attr
     deletion AND scoped deletion were both impossible.
   - generate_random_paths: missing source= (fixed walk start)
   - reconstruct_path: (sources, targets, pred) -> (source, target,
     predecessors); predecessors is now the nested floyd_warshall dict
     as nx expects (flat dijkstra-style still supported for backcompat).
   - partial_duplication_graph: fnx had (n, p, seed); nx has
     (N, n, p, q, seed, *, create_using). Rewrote to match, seeding
     with an n-clique and growing to N. duplication_divergence_graph
     keeps the simpler single-p walk so its (n, p) caller surface
     doesn't regress.

3. Round 39 cycle 3 (r39c3-01, LOW) — test bug:
   test_wl_subgraph_hashes_have_correct_length asserted 4 hashes for
   iterations=3 (comment 'init + 3 iterations'). nx returns exactly one
   hash per iteration; fnx correctly matched that already. Updated
   assertion to 3.

Also updates existing kosaraju test-suite callers to materialise the
generator via list() (nx's kosaraju_strongly_connected_components is a
generator function — the old fnx-style list-return broke parity) and
regenerates docs/coverage.md after the new Python wrappers shift three
functions from RUST_NATIVE to PY_WRAPPER (is_simple_path, is_isolate,
is_edge_cover, kosaraju_strongly_connected_components now go through
Python wrappers).

No new test regressions; two previously-failing tests now pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1218,3 +1218,6 @@
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-03","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] bethe_hessian_matrix: wrong return type AND wrong values","description":"Two defects in fnx.bethe_hessian_matrix: (1) returned a legacy scipy.sparse.csr_matrix while nx returns csr_array — isinstance(r, csr_array) was False. (2) Computed A = adjacency(G, weight=None) and regularizer r from A.sum(axis=1), both unweighted, but nx uses the *weighted* adjacency for A and D and the *unweighted* degrees for r. On karate_club_graph (integer edge weights) fnx diverged from nx by up to ~465 in matrix entries. Fixed by building A via to_scipy_sparse_array(G, nodelist, format='csr') (weighted by default), computing r from G.degree(v) per-node (unweighted), and using sparse eye_array/diags_array throughout so the final product is a csr_array. Verified on karate, cycle5, K4, havel_hakimi; spectrum matches too.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed type (csr_array) and values. Verified across weighted and unweighted fixtures; spectrum matches; existing tests pass."}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-04","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] laplacian / normalized_laplacian returned csr_matrix instead of csr_array","description":"fnx.laplacian_matrix and fnx.normalized_laplacian_matrix built their results with scipy.sparse.diags() and scipy.sparse.eye() (both returning legacy csr_matrix). networkx returns csr_array from its laplacian helpers, so isinstance(r, csr_array) was False on fnx. Fixed by switching to diags_array / eye_array and tocsr()-casting the final product. Verified on karate, cycle5, K4, path3 and weighted fixtures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: sparse-array helpers throughout, .tocsr() on final product; type and values match nx."}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-05","title":"REVIEW: [CRITICAL] floyd_warshall_numpy silently skipped weight-0 edges → wrong shortest distances","description":"fnx.floyd_warshall_numpy initialised its distance matrix by copying weights from to_numpy_array(G) but gated assignment on `if A[i,j] != 0`. to_numpy_array's default nonedge value is 0.0, so weight-0 edges were indistinguishable from absent edges and dropped from the initial distance matrix — leaving dist[i,j] = inf for any edge with weight 0. This silently corrupted shortest paths through zero-cost edges AND, on graphs with negative edges that resolved to a 0 intermediate, produced wrong final distances. Reproducer: DiGraph with edges (0,1,1),(1,2,1),(0,2,5),(1,3,-2),(3,2,0) — nx returns dist[0,2] = -1 (via 0->1->3->2) but fnx returned 2. Fixed by calling to_numpy_array with nonedge=np.inf and then copying the whole array — weight-0 edges stay finite and negative intermediate paths are preserved.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: nonedge=np.inf in to_numpy_array call, direct array copy. Verified on negative-edge DAG, zero-weight-chain DAG, karate (unweighted), and custom nodelist. All match nx exactly."}
+{"id":"br-r39-c1-01","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] 10+ more signature mismatches on public fnx surface (round 2 sweep)","description":"A second pass of the inspect.signature sweep surfaced more drop-in breakages that the Round 38 pass missed:
- is_simple_path: second positional named 'path' (from the Rust binding) instead of nx's 'nodes'
- is_isolate: second positional named 'node' instead of nx's 'n'
- is_edge_cover: second positional named 'edges' instead of nx's 'cover'
- all_neighbors: first positional named 'G' instead of nx's 'graph'
- to_directed / to_undirected: first positional named 'G' instead of nx's 'graph'
- restricted_view: named 'nodes_to_remove'/'edges_to_remove' instead of nx's 'nodes'/'edges'
- visibility_graph: named 'sequence' instead of nx's 'series'
- mycielskian: missing 'iterations' kwarg — nx supports iterated Mycielskians (chromatic number +k)
- kosaraju_strongly_connected_components: missing 'source' kwarg AND yielded lists instead of sets (nx returns sets — isinstance(s, set) was False on fnx)

All fixed with Python wrappers that accept the nx-named kwargs; kosaraju now coerces each Rust-side component to a set. Mycielskian now iterates the base transformation for ``iterations`` steps.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed signatures + return types. Each verified with nx-style kwarg calls; mycielskian iterations matches nx size (|V|=3 * 2^k + 2^(k-1) - 1 recurrence); kosaraju yields set instances; parity tests pass."}
+{"id":"br-r39-c2-01","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] 8 more signature / behaviour mismatches (attribute helpers, graph generators, reconstruct_path)","description":"Continuation of the Round 38/39 signature sweep. These surfaced as TypeError on nx-style kwarg calls:

- k_factor: missing matching_weight kwarg (nx uses it when k=1 to control the max-weight-matching tie-break)
- node_attribute_xy: missing nodes kwarg (source-side filter)
- remove_edge_attributes / remove_node_attributes: wrong signature — fnx took (G, name), nx takes (G, *attr_names, ebunch=None) / (G, *attr_names, nbunch=None). Multi-attr and scoped deletion both broken.
- generate_random_paths: missing source= kwarg (fixed walk start)
- reconstruct_path: signature was (sources, targets, pred); nx is (source, target, predecessors) and `predecessors` is normally the *nested* floyd_warshall dict {src: {tgt: predecessor}} rather than a flat dijkstra-style dict. Old fnx signature produced [] on the nested form.
- partial_duplication_graph: fnx had (n, p, seed); nx has (N, n, p, q, seed, *, create_using). Calling with N=10, n=3, p=0.5, q=0.1 raised TypeError. Rewrote to match nx (clique-seed of size n, grow to N, with both p and q probabilities).

All now accept the nx-named kwargs. reconstruct_path was generalised to handle both nested floyd_warshall dicts and flat dijkstra-style predecessor lists for backwards compat with existing fnx callers.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed signatures + behaviour. Verified each fixed function; duplication_divergence_graph kept on the original single-p walk so the (n,p) caller surface doesn't regress; partial_duplication_graph now matches nx (N,n,p,q)."}
+{"id":"br-r39-c3-01","title":"REVIEW: [LOW] WL subgraph-hashes test had outdated iterations+1 assertion","description":"test_wl_subgraph_hashes_have_correct_length asserted each node's hash list had len == 4 for iterations=3 (comment 'init + 3 iterations'). nx.weisfeiler_lehman_subgraph_hashes returns exactly one hash per iteration (no init entry); fnx's implementation correctly matches that contract. The test was on the pre-3.5 assumption. Updated assertion to 3.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Test updated. fnx.weisfeiler_lehman_subgraph_hashes already matched nx (3 hashes for iterations=3, verified against nx.path_graph(5))."}

@@ -7556,11 +7575,20 @@ def is_negatively_weighted(G, edge=None, weight="weight"):

 def max_weight_clique(G, weight="weight"):

@@ -8199,13 +8227,36 @@ def group_out_degree_centrality(G, S, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs):
     biconnected_components as _raw_biconnected_components,
     biconnected_component_edges,
     is_semiconnected as _raw_is_semiconnected,
-    kosaraju_strongly_connected_components,
+    kosaraju_strongly_connected_components as _raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components,
     attracting_components as _raw_attracting_components,
     number_attracting_components as _raw_number_attracting_components,
     is_attracting_component as _raw_is_attracting_component,
 )
 
 
+def kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(G, source=None):
+    """Kosaraju-style strongly connected components.
+
+    Each component is returned as a ``set`` to match networkx; the Rust
+    helper yields lists. ``source`` is accepted for networkx signature
+    parity — when provided, the component containing ``source`` is
+    yielded first, followed by the remaining SCCs in the Rust emission
+    order.
+    """
+    sccs = [set(component) for component in _raw_kosaraju_strongly_connected_components(G)]
+    if source is None:
+        yield from sccs
+        return
+    for component in sccs:
+        if source in component:
+            yield component
+            break
+    for component in sccs:
+        if source in component:
+            continue
+        yield component
+
+
 def node_connected_component(G, n):
     """Return the set of nodes in the connected component of ``n``.
 
@@ -9166,15 +9217,16 @@ def nodes_with_selfloops(G):
     return (node for node in G.adj if node in G.adj[node])
 
 
-def all_neighbors(G, node):
-    """Return all neighbors of *node* in *G* (including predecessors for DiGraph).
+def all_neighbors(graph, node):
+    """Return all neighbors of *node* in *graph* (predecessors + successors on DiGraph).
 
-    For undirected graphs, equivalent to ``G.neighbors(node)``.
+    First parameter named ``graph`` to match networkx's public signature.
+    For undirected graphs, equivalent to ``graph.neighbors(node)``.
     For directed graphs, returns predecessors followed by successors.
     """
-    if G.is_directed():
-        return itertools.chain(G.predecessors(node), G.successors(node))
-    return G.neighbors(node)
+    if graph.is_directed():
+        return itertools.chain(graph.predecessors(node), graph.successors(node))
+    return graph.neighbors(node)
 
 
 def is_path(G, path):
@@ -15128,38 +15180,59 @@ def all_pairs_all_shortest_paths(G, weight=None, method="dijkstra"):
         yield (source, paths_dict)
 
 
-def reconstruct_path(sources, targets, pred):
-    """Reconstruct a path from predecessors dict.
-
-    Parameters
-    ----------
-    sources : set of nodes
-    targets : set of nodes
-    pred : dict
-        Predecessor mapping.
+def reconstruct_path(source, target, predecessors):
+    """Reconstruct a path from *source* to *target*.
 
-    Returns
-    -------
-    list
-        The reconstructed path.
+    Matches networkx's public signature
+    ``reconstruct_path(source, target, predecessors)`` where
+    ``predecessors`` is either the nested
+    ``floyd_warshall_predecessor_and_distance`` dict
+    (``predecessors[src][tgt] -> predecessor``) or a flat dict of
+    ``{node: predecessor_list}`` (dijkstra-style output).
     """
-    for target in targets:
+    # Float-warshall style: predecessors is {src: {tgt: pred}}
+    if (
+        isinstance(predecessors, dict)
+        and source in predecessors
+        and isinstance(predecessors.get(source), dict)
+    ):
+        table = predecessors[source]
+        if source == target:
+            return []
+        if target not in table:
+            return []
         path = [target]
         current = target
-        while current not in sources:
-            preds = pred.get(current, [])
-            if not preds:
-                break
-            current = preds[0]
+        while current != source:
+            current = table[current]
             path.append(current)
-        if current in sources:
-            path.reverse()
-            return path
-    return []
+        path.reverse()
+        return path
+
+    # Dijkstra-style: predecessors is {node: [pred1, ...]}
+    if source == target:
+        return [source]
+    path = [target]
+    current = target
+    while current != source:
+        preds = predecessors.get(current, [])
+        if not preds:
+            return []
+        current = preds[0] if isinstance(preds, (list, tuple)) else preds
+        path.append(current)
+    path.reverse()
+    return path

+    G,

+    *,
+    source=None,
 ):
     """Generate random paths by random walks.

@@ -18238,9 +18317,17 @@ def ra_index_soundarajan_hopcroft(G, ebunch=None, community="community"):
         yield (u, v, score)
 
 
-def node_attribute_xy(G, attribute):
-    """Yield (x, y) pairs of attribute values for edges."""
+def node_attribute_xy(G, attribute, nodes=None):
+    """Yield (x, y) pairs of attribute values for edges.
+
+    ``nodes`` restricts the source side: when given, only edges whose
+    source is in ``nodes`` are emitted. Matches networkx's public
+    signature ``node_attribute_xy(G, attribute, nodes=None)``.
+    """
+    node_filter = None if nodes is None else set(nodes)
     for u, nbrsdict in G.adjacency():
+        if node_filter is not None and u not in node_filter:
+            continue
         u_attrs = G.nodes[u] if hasattr(G.nodes, "__getitem__") else {}
         x = u_attrs.get(attribute) if isinstance(u_attrs, dict) else None
         if G.is_multigraph():
@@ -18335,19 +18422,61 @@ def recursive_simple_cycles(G):
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 
 
-def remove_node_attributes(G, name):
-    """Remove attribute *name* from all nodes."""
-    for node in G.nodes():
+def remove_node_attributes(G, *attr_names, nbunch=None):
+    """Remove the given attribute names from nodes in *nbunch* (default all).
+
+    Matches networkx's public signature
+    ``remove_node_attributes(G, *attr_names, nbunch=None)``.
+    """
+    if not attr_names:
+        # Treat absence as no-op (matches nx silently doing nothing).
+        return
+    target_nodes = G.nodes() if nbunch is None else [n for n in nbunch if n in G]
+    for node in target_nodes:
         attrs = G.nodes[node] if hasattr(G.nodes, "__getitem__") else {}
-        if isinstance(attrs, dict) and name in attrs:
-            del attrs[name]
+        if not isinstance(attrs, dict):
+            continue
+        for name in attr_names:
+            if name in attrs:
+                del attrs[name]
 
 
-def remove_edge_attributes(G, name):
-    """Remove attribute *name* from all edges."""
-    for u, v, data in G.edges(data=True):
-        if isinstance(data, dict) and name in data:
-            del data[name]
+def remove_edge_attributes(G, *attr_names, ebunch=None):
+    """Remove the given attribute names from edges in *ebunch* (default all).
+
+    Matches networkx's public signature
+    ``remove_edge_attributes(G, *attr_names, ebunch=None)``.
+    """
+    if not attr_names:
+        return
+    if ebunch is None:
+        edge_iter = list(G.edges(keys=True) if G.is_multigraph() else G.edges())
+    else:
+        edge_iter = list(ebunch)
+    for edge in edge_iter:
+        if G.is_multigraph() and len(edge) == 3:
+            u, v, k = edge
+            if G.has_edge(u, v, k):
+                data = G[u][v][k]
+            else:
+                continue
+        else:
+            u, v = edge[0], edge[1]
+            if G.has_edge(u, v):
+                data = G[u][v]
+                if G.is_multigraph():
+                    # No key provided on MG — nx strips across all parallel edges.
+                    for k in list(data.keys()):
+                        d = data[k]
+                        if isinstance(d, dict):
+                            for name in attr_names:
+                                d.pop(name, None)
+                    continue
+            else:
+                continue
+        if isinstance(data, dict):
+            for name in attr_names:
+                data.pop(name, None)
 
 
 def floyd_warshall_numpy(G, nodelist=None, weight="weight"):
@@ -18604,14 +18733,22 @@ def _chordless_cycles_via_parity(G, *, length_bound=None):
     )
 
 
-def to_undirected(G):
-    """Return a frozen live undirected view of G."""
-    return _generic_undirected_graph_view(G)
+def to_undirected(graph):
+    """Return a frozen live undirected view of *graph*.
